encounterLog.description: A narrow trail bends out of sight ahead. The forest leans close, and the only sound is your own breath.
The smell of brine and old earth hangs thick. Somewhere among the broken walls, wings stir that shouldn't.

Warm light and the smell of ale spill out as you push through the door of the Rusty Flagon. The common room is busy — locals hunched over their drinks at scattered tables, low conversation filling the smoky air. Behind the long bar counter to the north, Bram Holdfast wipes down a tankard without looking up. He always knows who's walked in.
The crossroads should be quiet at this hour. It is not.

Mist clings to the forest floor and a hush rides on the breeze — the kind of quiet that follows when every animal has gone still at once. To the south where the path opens, you can still see daylight; ahead, the canopy crowds together and a faint coppery smell drifts on the air. Somewhere deeper in, a low rhythmic chant rises and falls.
The smell of brine and old earth hangs thick. Somewhere among the broken walls, wings stir that shouldn't.

A narrow stair drops away beneath the broken flagstones of an old roadside chapel, and the air rising from below is colder than the night above. Tallow-smoke clings to your cloak as you descend. Three chambers lie ahead — antechamber, vestibule, and a deeper sanctum where lamplight flickers against carved stone. From somewhere within, the faintest thread of voices threads through the dark, rising and falling in a cadence that does not belong to any language you remember.

lines.text: You catch a thread of voices through the stone — a low, ritual chant from somewhere north.
The cultists break off their chant. One turns toward you, knife still in hand. "Turn around. You have no business here."
Turn order: Aldric Vane → Cultist (A) → Cultist (B)
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
Graze with Greatsword — 3 slashing  [d20(5)+5=10 vs AC 12]
Hit with Ritual Sickle — 2 slashing  [d20(15)+3=18 vs AC 17 · 1d4[1]+1]
Hit with Ritual Sickle — 2 slashing  [d20(16)+3=19 vs AC 17 · 1d4[1]+1]
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
Graze with Greatsword — 3 slashing  [d20(6)+5=11 vs AC 12]
Miss with Ritual Sickle  [d20(6)+3=9 vs AC 17]
Hit with Ritual Sickle — 5 slashing  [d20(16)+3=19 vs AC 17 · 1d4[4]+1]
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
⚡ Critical hit with Greatsword — 17 slashing  [d20(20)+5=25 vs AC 12 · 2×2d6[3,4,4,3]+3]
Hit with Ritual Sickle — 2 slashing  [d20(17)+3=20 vs AC 17 · 1d4[1]+1]
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
⚡ Critical hit with Greatsword — 22 slashing  [d20(20)+5=25 vs AC 12 · 2×2d6[5,4,5,5]+3]

encounterLog.description: Mist clings to the forest floor and a hush rides on the breeze — the kind of quiet that follows when every animal has gone still at once. To the south where the path opens, you can still see daylight; ahead, the canopy crowds together and a faint coppery smell drifts on the air. Somewhere deeper in, a low rhythmic chant rises and falls.

You and a frightened traveller stand at the near end of a narrow stone bridge spanning a dark gorge. Two cloaked figures block the far end — armed, still, and watching. One steps forward and raises a hand. "Ten gold," he growls, "or you don't cross." The traveller edges close beside you and whispers: "I'm with you — whatever you decide."

lines.text: "Oi! Where d'you think you're going?" Both bandits step onto the bridge, weapons rising.
Turn order: Aldric Vane → Bridge Bandit (B) → Bridge Bandit (A) → Frightened Traveller
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
Bridge Bandit (B) is out of reach
Bridge Bandit (A) is out of reach
Frightened Traveller moves but cannot reach the enemy
── Aldric Vane's turn — Action & Bonus refreshed ──
⚡ Critical hit with Greatsword — 14 slashing  [d20(20)+5=25 vs AC 12 · 2×2d6[1,1,6,3]+3]
Aldric Vane uses Action Surge — additional Action this turn (0/1 left until short rest)
Hit with Greatsword — 11 slashing  [d20(17)+5=22 vs AC 12 · 2d6[5,3]+3 Savage]

Behind the long bar counter to the north, Bram Holdfast wipes down a tankard without looking up. He always knows who's walked in.

You and a frightened traveller stand at the near end of a narrow stone bridge spanning a dark gorge. Two cloaked figures block the far end — armed, still, and watching. One steps forward and raises a hand. "Ten gold," he growls, "or you don't cross." The traveller edges close beside you and whispers: "I'm with you — whatever you decide."

lines.text: The lead bandit spits over the rail. "Cheap bastard. Have it your way then." They move to intercept.
